title: Llama7B attention source-backed HBM energy calibration
hypothesis: The merged HBM/DRAM service-energy result uses explicit command-class pJ parameters, but those values are not source calibrated. Recomputing the selected Llama7B frontier against source-backed aggregate HBM pJ/bit references should expose whether the energy-best point and dominant energy component remain stable.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does source-backed aggregate HBM pJ/bit calibration preserve or move the Llama7B HBM/DRAM service-energy frontier?
include: consume the merged HBM/DRAM service-energy result
consume HBM2/HBM2E/HBM3 aggregate energy references from the design registry
recompute HBM energy for retained service-model frontier rows
report latency-best, energy-best, dominant energy component, and remaining abstractions under each source-backed HBM energy anchor
follow_on_broad_sweep: If calibrated HBM dominates or moves the point, replace aggregate pJ/bit bounds with a controller/current-calibrated HBM model.
If the point remains stable, measure or bound compute energy at the selected MAC/cycle target.

expected_benefit: Replaces unsourced HBM pJ assumptions with explicit source-backed memory-energy anchors.
Prevents claiming compute or HBM dominance before the dominant memory term has a literature-backed range.
Keeps the result revisable by storing the external reference measurements in the design registry.
risks: External HBM pJ/bit references are aggregate access-energy anchors, not command-class stack-current signoff.
The calibration preserves the existing HBM/DRAM service latency model and does not add cycle-accurate controller simulation.
Compute energy remains scaled from a nearest measured dense compute reference.

required_evaluations.objective: Calibrate retained Llama7B HBM/DRAM service-energy frontier rows against source-backed aggregate HBM pJ/bit references.

expected_result.reason: Determine whether source-backed aggregate HBM energy changes the current Llama7B service-energy frontier before final energy ranking.
